items.title: Serena Williams announces return to professional tennis with Queen's Club doubles wildcard
items.summary: The greatest women's champion of the Open Era is returning to tennis after almost four years away, accepting a wildcard into the doubles at the Queen's Club tournament next week.

items.title: Council uses AI to inform 10-year plan for 'more depth' at 'fifth of the price'
items.summary: A Tasmanian council says using AI for a strategic plan has saved them time and money, and will be used much more in the future, but a resident says the community consultation is 'completely lost with AI'.

items.title: Laos cave rescue team member's journey from soldier to comedian to diver
items.summary: Josh Richards has made news for his diving and his interplanetary ambitions. Now he's using his skills to try to rescue others.

items.title: 'Daniel' is a fake AI real estate agent who is trying to sell units to strangers
items.summary: "Daniel" is an AI real estate agent who calls strangers and secretly records conversations without their consent.

items.title: Former wife of tobacco baron alleges horrific life entangled in illegal trade
items.summary: After an arranged marriage to tobacco baron Sam Albion, Kawther Salih says the relationship spiralled into an abusive situation that saw her subjugated into working in the underground tobacco industry.

items.title: Emirates proposes increase in helicopter flights at luxury Wolgan Valley resort
items.summary: A luxury resort that has been shut for three years hopes to reopen by chauffeuring guests down a steep 4WD track and flying them in on more than 200 helicopter flights a week.

items.title: Voters consider alternatives to major political parties as One Nation popularity surges
items.summary: Pauline Hanson's right-wing populist party is leading opinion polls for the first time, and regional Queenslanders say they can see why.

items.title: Birds return to Little Dog Island in Bass Strait after feral cats eradicated
items.summary: Penguins have been sighted on a remote Bass Strait island for the first time in decades after the successful eradication of feral cats and possums.

items.title: Antibiotic florfenicol detected at 'low level' 14km from fish farm site where it was used
items.summary: Tasmania's Natural Resources and Environment Department has confirmed the antibiotic florfenicol was detected at a 'low level' in wild seafood 14 kilometres from where the drug was administered.

items.title: Even Hanson knows polling isn't everything but major parties have their work cut out
items.summary: The One Nation leader, whose popularity has soared in the past 12 months, would be the first to say she doesn't put too much stock in the numbers.

items.title: ATO warns millions of Australian chasing tax deductions to stop making 'unusual' claims
items.summary: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) is warning Australians to stop claiming private expenses on their tax returns. The agency gives its tax-time tips, including the dos and don'ts of claiming work-related expenses and investment losses.

items.title: How batteries muscled out gas and pushed power prices down
items.summary: Fossil fuels, which have powered the global economy for centuries, are under threat. And Australia has become ground zero in this global battle.

items.title: When police charged footballer Tom Starling with assault, one detective broke ranks
items.summary: As the footballer, shirt torn, face bloodied and with a concussion, paced his police cell, a veteran detective's phone started to ring.

items.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything
items.summary: Experts say if a fight keeps ending up in the bedroom, it could be a sign you and your partner aren't resolving anything.

items.title: Carly Sheppard unites with Alisdair Macindoe for The Shepherds at the Australian Dance Biennale
items.summary: Carly Sheppard is perhaps best known for experimental performance, but a coincidental meeting with a fellow dancer convinced her to go back to her roots.

items.title: Scripps Spelling Bee quiz: Do you know what these words mean?
items.summary: We don't think you'll have even heard of most of these words, let alone know how to spell them.

items.title: Iranian state media reports peace talks with US suspended but Trump insists they continue at 'rapid pace'
items.summary: Iranian state media is reporting that the country's government has halted negotiations with the United States over an end to the war in the Middle East, in protest over Israel's conduct in Lebanon.

items.title: Oxfam research finds record number of Australian billionaires hold total wealth of $686b
items.summary: Australia now has 178 billionaires who collectively hold more than $686 billion in wealth, sparking debate about the advantages and disadvantages of the growing number of ultra-wealthy.

items.title: The disturbing role of ChatGPT in mass shootings

items.summary: The large pooch swam 800 metres to a nearby island after being swept off rocks at Batemans Bay.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: 'Awestruck' Grenvold bolts into the Diamonds squad

items.summary: It was a "boys' trip to the races" that went wrong, a Supreme Court jury in Toowoomba has heard.

items.summary: Queensland Police are investigating after a nine-week-old baby was taken to hospital earlier this year with "serious" head injuries.

items.title: Mark Sheridan Waden faces trial over alleged murder of US beauty queen Priscilla Brooten
items.summary: The murder trial of Mark Sheridan Waden has heard allegations he killed his then-partner Priscilla Brooten during an argument almost eight years ago, after the American former beauty queen learned he was having an affair with a colleague.

items.summary: Queensland's task of levelling the State of Origin series has become more difficult after confirmation two of its stars have been ruled out of Game II at the MCG.

items.title: NT WorkSafe charges health department over death of mental health patient at RDH
items.summary: WorkSafe charges Northern Territory Health with failing to comply with health and safety duties over the death of a mental health in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital in 2022.

items.title: International Ebola cases climbing 'fast' but Australia won't impose travel restrictions
items.summary: The federal government will not impose border restrictions on travellers from Ebola-stricken countries despite new suspected cases in Italy and Brazil, as the worsening outbreak prompts other countries to take action.

items.summary: Australia puts itself in pole position to win the women's rugby sevens World Championship, while the men's team also enjoys the spoils of victory in Spain.

items.title: $6 million of cocaine found near burnt-out truck at boat ramp in Whitsundays
items.summary: Packages of cocaine estimated to be worth $6 million have been found floating in the sea and near the remains of a burnt-out truck at an isolated north Queensland boat ramp.

items.title: Tasmania's greyhound racing ban won't go to upper house vote until success assured, premier says
items.summary: A bill to ban greyhound racing in Tasmania has been delayed until the government can secure enough support to pass it. Meanwhile, financial support has been offered to racing participants ahead of the 2029 phase-out deadline.

items.summary: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

items.title: Chadstone cold case accused Garry Sullivan faces court three decades after armed robbery

items.title: Adelaide Hills man Bo De-Simone pleads guilty to murder of mother Linda Simon
items.summary: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.

items.title: Thousands of baby green sea turtles head to sea after successful egg relocation
items.summary: The project at Raine Island, where 90 per cent of northern green sea turtles nest, has been working for a decade to improve hatching success.

items.title: Memory failure contributed to deaths of children left in hot cars, NSW coroner finds

items.title: Mass flowering of Gebang palms in Darwin described as 'once-in-a-lifetime' natural event
items.summary: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

items.title: Tourism operators say AI videos spread 'wrong impression' of WA's north

items.title: House fire allegedly started by half-brother of murdered teen Angus Beaumont

items.title: Public servant to take SA government to court over North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment
items.summary: An Adelaide man alleges the state government failed to properly refer the controversial North Adelaide Golf Course to the federal environment minister for approval.

items.title: Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's French Open dinner for black players part of 'an old tradition'
items.summary: From the Harlem Renaissance of the 20s and 30s to Soul Food Sundays at Wimbledon in the 80s and 90s, Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party before the French Open continues a long history of black athletes coming together.

items.title: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, says government is failing on First Nations policy
items.summary: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, saying she has lost faith in the territory government's approach to Indigenous affairs.

items.title: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff faces estimates scrutiny after resignation of minister Madeleine Ogilvie
items.summary: "You can ask it in 50 different ways … I won't be commenting any further", the Tasmanian premier says as he responds to questioning in parliament from Labor and the Greens.

items.title: Greens stalwart Bob Brown joins fight against small South West sand mine
items.summary: The prominent environmentalist declares the sand mine project "an industrial nightmare" after visiting the area.

items.title: Police defend actions after dog run over then shot dead by officers
items.summary: A North West Queensland woman says her family has been left traumatised after police shot her granddaughter's injured dog in a residential street.

items.title: Antisemitism royal commission rejects federal government's bid to keep cabinet documents confidential
items.summary: Commissioner Virginia Bell says the documents will provide a comparison of the resourcing given to counterterrorism agencies before and after August 2024, when the terror threat level was raised to "probable".

items.title: Large flock of critically endangered swift parrots seen near Port Macquarie
items.summary: The sighting of critically endangered parrots is seen as "really exciting" after hardly any were seen across NSW last year. But a bird expert warns the species still faces significant threats.

items.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City wins FA Cup, beating Dario Vidošić's Brighton and Hove Albion
items.summary: The Women's FA Cup has a distinctly Australian flavour with Mary Fowler's Manchester City thumping Charlize Rule's Brighton and Hove Albion, coached by compatriot Dario Vidošić, whose father was honoured by fans at Wembley.

items.title: Joao Fonseca reaches first grand slam quarterfinal while Iga Świątek is out of French Open
